2606.19494.title: Hidden Anchors in Multi-Agent LLM Deliberation
2606.19494.paper_summary: Multi-agent LLM deliberation, where agents exchange and revise answers over several rounds, is increasingly used to improve reasoning and accuracy, yet how and why it works is rarely modelled. Such deliberation mirrors how humans reach decisions. As social animals we are pulled both by the group, the herd effect that classical opinion-dynamics models such as DeGroot and Friedkin--Johnsen capture, and by our own internal belief, which they do not. We model multi-agent deliberation as a closed-loop dynamical system in which each agent carries a hidden internal belief, its anchor, that continually pulls its opinion regardless of its neighbours. We show this anchor can be recovered from the deliberation alone, and that it explains a behaviour classical consensus rules forbid: an agent's confidence in the correct answer can climb past where any agent started, escaping the space (convexhull) formed by the initial beliefs. Checking whether the recovered anchor also predicts held-out runs (generalizes) gives a simple test for when a model is truly driven bysuch an anchor. Across three open-weight model families this is a spectrum, not all-or-nothing. All anchors' influence are about equally strongly, but they differ in where the anchor sits, and only when it sits far from the initial opinions does deliberation escape the hull and need the full closed-loop model.
2606.19494.paper_summary_zh: 多代理 LLM 深思熟慮，代理之間在幾輪中交換和修訂答案，越來越被用來提高推理和準確性，但它是如何運作的以及為什麼有效卻很少被建模。這種深思熟慮反映了人類如何做出決策。作為社會性動物，我們受到群體的影響，即經典意見動態模型如 DeGroot 和 Friedkin--Johnsen 所捕捉的羊群效應，以及我們自身的內在信念，這一點它們並未考慮。我們將多代理深思熟慮建模為一個閉環動態系統，其中每個代理都擁有一個隱藏的內部信念，即其錨點，這個錨點不斷拉動其意見，無論鄰居的意見如何。我們顯示這個錨點可以僅從深思熟慮中恢復出來，並且它解釋了一種經典共識規則所禁止的行為：代理對正確答案的信心可以超過任何代理的起始點，逃離由初始信念形成的空間（凸包）。檢查恢復的錨點是否也能預測保留的運行（泛化）提供了一個簡單的測試，以判斷模型是否真正受到這樣的錨點驅動。在三個開放權重模型系列中，這是一個光譜，而非全有或全無。所有錨點的影響力大致相等，但它們在錨點的位置上有所不同，只有當錨點遠離初始意見時，深思熟慮才能逃離凸包並需要完整的閉環模型。
2606.19494.author: Apurba Pokharel et.al.
